This industry comprises establishments of independent representatives primarily engaged in selling media time or space for media owners.
See industry description for 541850.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in creating and designing public display advertising campaign materials, such as printed, painted, or electronic displays; and/or placing such displays on indoor or outdoor billboards and panels, or on or within transit vehicles or facilities, shopping malls, retail (in-store) displays, and other display structures or sites.
See industry description for 541860.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in (1) creating and designing advertising campaigns for the purpose of distributing advertising materials (e.g., coupons, flyers, samples) or specialties (e.g., keychains, magnets, pens with customized messages imprinted) by mail or other direct distribution and/or (2) preparing such advertising materials or specialties for mailing or other direct distribution. These establishments may also compile, maintain, sell, and rent mailing lists.
See industry description for 541870.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in the direct distribution or delivery of advertisements (e.g., circulars, coupons, handbills) or samples. Establishments in this industry use methods, such as delivering advertisements or samples door-to-door, placing flyers or coupons on car windshields in parking lots, or handing out samples in retail stores.

This industry provides advertising services (excluding advertising agencies, public relations, media buying, media representative, display advertising, direct mail, and advertising material distribution services). It includes advertising specialties distribution, sign lettering, display lettering, store window dressing, and merchandise demonstration services.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing advertising services (except advertising agency services, public relations agency services, media buying agency services, media representative services, display advertising services, direct mail advertising services, advertising material distribution services, and marketing consulting services).

naics_name: Other Professional, Scientific, and Technical Services
naics_desc: This industry group provides professional, scientific, or technical services (excluding legal, accounting, architectural, engineering, design, computer, management, R&D, advertising, market research, photographic, translation, and veterinary services). It includes appraisal, arbitration, commodity inspection, consumer credit counseling, and weather forecasting.

naics_name: Marketing Research and Public Opinion Polling
naics_desc: This industry systematically gathers, records, tabulates, and presents marketing and public opinion data. It includes broadcast media rating services, political opinion polling, marketing analysis/research, and statistical sampling services.

naics_name: Marketing Research and Public Opinion Polling
naics_desc: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in systematically gathering, recording, tabulating, and presenting marketing and public opinion data.

This industry provides still, video, or digital photography services, specializing in fields like commercial/industrial photography, portrait photography, and special events. It includes commercial/portrait photography studios and videotaping services for events.
This U.S. industry comprises establishments known as portrait studios primarily engaged in providing still, video, or digital portrait photography services.
This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing commercial photography services, generally for advertising agencies, publishers, and other business and industrial users.
This industry translates written material and interprets speech from one language to another, and/or provides sign language services.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in translating written material and interpreting speech from one language to another and establishments primarily engaged in providing sign language services.

This industry comprises licensed veterinary practitioners practicing veterinary medicine, dentistry, or surgery for animals. It also includes establishments providing testing services for these practitioners, e.g., animal hospitals and veterinary clinics.

This industry comprises establishments of licensed veterinary practitioners primarily engaged in the practice of veterinary medicine, dentistry, or surgery for animals; and establishments primarily engaged in providing testing services for licensed veterinary practitioners.

naics_name: All Other Professional, Scientific, and Technical Services
naics_desc: This industry provides professional, scientific, or technical services (excluding legal, accounting, architectural, engineering, design, computer, management, R&D, advertising, market research, photographic, translation, and veterinary services). It includes appraisal, arbitration, commodity inspection, consumer credit counseling, and weather forecasting.

naics_name: All Other Professional, Scientific, and Technical Services
naics_desc: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in the provision of professional, scientific, or technical services (except legal services; accounting, tax preparation, bookkeeping, and related services; architectural, engineering, and related services; specialized design services; computer systems design and related services; management, scientific, and technical consulting services; scientific research and development services; advertising, public relations, and related services; market research and public opinion polling; photographic services; translation and interpretation services; and veterinary services).

This sector includes holding companies with controlling interests and establishments that administer, oversee, and manage other company entities, performing strategic planning and decision-making roles. Excludes government establishments and day-to-day office administrative services.

This subsector includes holding companies that maintain controlling interests in other firms, and establishments that administer, oversee, and manage company entities, engaging in strategic or organizational planning and decision-making.

This industry group includes holding companies that hold securities for controlling interest or influencing management, and establishments that administer, oversee, and manage other entities of the company/enterprise, undertaking strategic planning and decision-making.

This industry includes legal entities (holding companies) holding securities for controlling interest/influencing management, and establishments (excluding government) administering/overseeing/managing other company entities, undertaking strategic/organizational planning and decision-making.

This U.S. industry comprises legal entities known as bank holding companies primarily engaged in holding the securities of (or other equity interests in) companies and enterprises for the purpose of owning a controlling interest or influencing the management decisions of these firms. The holding companies in this industry do not administer, oversee, and manage other establishments of the company or enterprise whose securities they hold.

This U.S. industry comprises legal entities known as holding companies (except bank holding) primarily engaged in holding the securities of (or other equity interests in) companies and enterprises for the purpose of owning a controlling interest or influencing the management decisions of these firms. The holding companies in this industry do not administer, oversee, and manage other establishments of the company or enterprise whose securities they hold.

naics_name: Corporate, Subsidiary, and Regional Managing Offices
naics_desc: This U.S. industry comprises establishments (except government establishments) primarily engaged in administering, overseeing, and managing other establishments of the company or enterprise. These establishments normally undertake the strategic or organizational planning and decision-making role of the company or enterprise. Establishments in this industry may hold the securities of the company or enterprise.

naics_name: Administrative and Support and Waste Management and Remediation Services
naics_desc: This sector provides routine support activities for other organizations, including office administration, personnel placement, document preparation, security, cleaning, and waste disposal services, typically on a contract basis. It covers both administrative support and waste management functions.

This subsector provides activities supporting the day-to-day operations of other organizations, specializing in functions like general management, personnel administration, clerical work, and cleaning. Services are typically contract- or fee-based, and include travel arrangement.
This industry group provides day-to-day office administrative services, e.g., financial planning, billing, recordkeeping, personnel, physical distribution, and logistics, on a contract or fee basis.

This industry provides a range of day-to-day office administrative services on a contract/fee basis, e.g., financial planning, billing, recordkeeping, personnel, physical distribution, and logistics, without providing operating staff for complete business operations.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing a range of day-to-day office administrative services, such as financial planning; billing and recordkeeping; personnel; and physical distribution and logistics, for others on a contract or fee basis. These establishments do not provide operating staff to carry out the complete operations of a business.

This industry group provides operating staff for a combination of support services within a client's facilities, e.g., janitorial, maintenance, trash disposal, security, mail routing, and reception, without managing the client's core business.

This industry provides operating staff for a combination of support services within a client's facilities, e.g., janitorial, maintenance, trash disposal, guard/security, mail routing, and reception, without responsibility for the client's core business. It includes private jail services.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing operating staff to perform a combination of support services within a client's facilities. Establishments in this industry typically provide a combination of services, such as janitorial, maintenance, trash disposal, guard and security, mail routing, reception, laundry, and related services to support operations within facilities. These establishments provide operating staff to carry out these support activities, but are not involved with or responsible for the core business or activities of the client. Establishments providing facilities (except computer and/or data processing) operation support services and establishments providing private jail services or operating correctional facilities (i.e., jails) on a contract or fee basis are included in this industry.

This industry group lists employment vacancies, refers/places applicants, provides executive search/recruitment, and supplies temporary workers to clients. It also offers human resources and human resource management services to businesses/households.

naics_name: Employment Placement Agencies and Executive Search Services
naics_desc: This industry lists employment vacancies, recruits, refers, or places applicants, and/or provides executive search, recruitment, and placement services for executive/senior management positions. The individuals placed are not employees of the agencies.

This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in listing employment vacancies and in recruiting, referring, or placing applicants for employment. The individuals referred or placed are not employees of the employment agencies.

This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing executive search, recruitment, and placement services for clients with specific executive and senior management position requirements. The range of services provided by these establishments may include developing a search strategy and position specification based on the culture and needs of the client; researching, identifying, screening, and interviewing candidates; verifying candidate qualifications; and assisting in final offer negotiations and assimilation of the selected candidate. The individuals identified, recruited, or placed are not employees of the executive search services establishments.

This industry supplies workers to clients' businesses for limited periods to supplement the workforce. Individuals are employees of the temporary help service, but direct supervision is at the client's site.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in supplying workers to clients' businesses for limited periods of time to supplement the working force of the client. The individuals provided are employees of the temporary help services establishment. However, these establishments do not provide direct supervision of their employees at the clients' work sites.

This industry provides human resources and HR management services to clients in a co-employment relationship, specializing in payroll, payroll tax, benefits administration, workers' compensation, and HR administration.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing human resources and human resource management services to client businesses and households. Establishments in this industry operate in a co-employment relationship with client businesses or organizations and are specialized in performing a wide range of human resource and personnel management duties, such as payroll, payroll tax, benefits administration, workers' compensation, unemployment, and human resource administration. Professional employer organizations (PEOs) are responsible for payroll, including withholding and remitting employment-related taxes, for some or all of the employees of their clients, and also serve as the employer of those employees for benefits and related purposes.

This industry group performs ongoing routine business support functions for clients on a contract/fee basis or for other establishments within the same enterprise. It includes document preparation, telephone call centers, private mail services, copy shops, collection agencies, and credit bureaus.

This industry writes letters/resumes, edits/proofreads documents, performs typing/word processing/desktop publishing, and provides stenography (excluding court reporting), transcription, and other secretarial services.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in one or more of the following: (1) letter or resume writing; (2) document editing or proofreading; (3) typing, word processing, or desktop publishing; and (4) stenography (except court reporting or stenotype recording), transcription, and other secretarial services.

This industry operates call centers that initiate or receive communications via telephone, facsimile, email for promoting products/services, taking orders, soliciting contributions, or providing information/assistance. Services are on behalf of clients or for other establishments of the same enterprise.
This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in answering telephone calls and relaying messages on behalf of clients or for other establishments of the same enterprise.

naics_name: Telemarketing Bureaus and Other Contact Centers
naics_desc: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in operating call centers that initiate or receive communications via telephone, facsimile, email, or other communication modes for purposes such as: (1) promoting products or services, (2) taking orders, (3) soliciting contributions, and (4) providing information or assistance regarding products or services. Telemarketing bureaus and other contact centers provide these services on behalf of clients and do not own the products or provide the services that they are representing, or they serve other establishments of the same enterprise.

This industry provides mailbox rental/postal/mailing services (excluding direct mail advertising), photocopying/duplicating/blueprinting/document copying (excluding printing), and a range of office support services (excluding printing), e.g., facsimile, word processing, on-site PC rental, and office product sales.

This U.S. industry comprises (1) establishments primarily engaged in providing mailbox rental and other postal and mailing (except direct mail advertising) services or (2) establishments engaged in providing these mailing services along with one or more other office support services, such as facsimile services, word processing services, on-site PC rental services, and office product sales.

naics_name: Other Business Service Centers (including Copy Shops)
naics_desc: This U.S. industry comprises (1) establishments generally known as copy centers or shops primarily engaged in providing photocopying, duplicating, blueprinting, and other document copying services, without also providing printing services (e.g., offset printing, quick printing, digital printing, prepress services) and (2) establishments (except private mail centers) engaged in providing a range of office support services (except printing services), such as document copying services, facsimile services, word processing services, on-site PC rental services, and office product sales.
This industry collects payments for claims and remits collected payments to clients, e.g., account/delinquent account collection services and tax collection services on contract/fee basis.
This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in collecting payments for claims and remitting payments collected to their clients.
This industry compiles information (e.g., credit/employment histories) and provides it to financial institutions, retailers, and others to evaluate individuals'/businesses' creditworthiness.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in compiling information, such as credit and employment histories, and providing the information to financial institutions, retailers, and others who have a need to evaluate the creditworthiness of individuals and businesses.

This industry provides business support services (excluding secretarial, telephone answering/telemarketing, private mail, document copying, debt collection, credit reporting, repossession, and court reporting). It includes address bar coding, charitable fundraising campaign organization, and mail presorting.

This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in repossessing tangible assets (e.g., automobiles, boats, equipment, planes, furniture, appliances) for the creditor as a result of delinquent debts.
This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing verbatim reporting and stenotype recording of live legal proceedings and transcribing subsequent recorded materials.

This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing business support services (except secretarial and other document preparation services; telephone answering and telemarketing services; private mail services or document copying services conducted as separate activities or in conjunction with other office support services; monetary debt collection services; credit reporting services; repossession services; and court reporting and stenotype recording services).

This industry group provides travel arrangement and reservation services, including travel agency services, tour operation, and other related services like hotel/restaurant reservations and ticket sales for various events.
This industry acts as agents in selling travel, tour, and accommodation services to the general public and commercial clients.
This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in acting as agents in selling travel, tour, and accommodation services to the general public and commercial clients.
This industry arranges and assembles tours, which are sold through travel agencies or other tour operators. It includes travel and wholesale tour operators.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in arranging and assembling tours. The tours are sold through travel agencies or tour operators. Travel or wholesale tour operators are included in this industry.

naics_name: Other Travel Arrangement and Reservation Services
naics_desc: This industry provides travel arrangement and reservation services (excluding travel agencies and tour operators). It includes condominium time-share exchange, road/travel services automobile clubs, convention/visitors bureaus, ticket agencies (amusement, sports, theatrical), and reservation services (airline, car rental, hotel, restaurant).

This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in marketing and promoting communities and facilities to businesses and leisure travelers through a range of activities, such as assisting organizations in locating meeting and convention sites; providing travel information on area attractions, lodging accommodations, restaurants; providing maps; and organizing group tours of local historical, recreational, and cultural attractions.

naics_name: All Other Travel Arrangement and Reservation Services
naics_desc: This U.S. industry comprises establishments (except travel agencies, tour operators, and convention and visitors bureaus) primarily engaged in providing travel arrangement and reservation services.

This industry group provides investigation, guard, and armored car services. It includes detective work, personal background checks, guard/patrol services, money/receipt transit protection, selling/installing/repairing security systems, and remote monitoring of alarms.

naics_name: Investigation, Guard, and Armored Car Services
naics_desc: This industry provides investigation, detective, and personal background check services; guard and patrol services (e.g., bodyguard, guard dog, parking security); and armored car services for picking up/delivering money/valuables.

naics_name: Investigation and Personal Background Check Services
naics_desc: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing investigation, detective, and personal background check services.
This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing guard and patrol services, such as bodyguard, guard dog, and parking security services.

This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in picking up and delivering money, receipts, or other valuable items. These establishments maintain personnel and equipment to protect such properties while in transit.

This industry sells security systems (e.g., burglar/fire alarms, locking devices, safes, security vaults) with installation, repair, or monitoring services, and/or remotely monitors electronic security alarm systems.

This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in (1) selling security alarm systems, such as burglar and fire alarms, along with installation, repair, or monitoring services or (2) remote monitoring of electronic security alarm systems.

This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in (1) selling mechanical or electronic locking devices, safes, and security vaults, along with installation, repair, rebuilding, or adjusting services or (2) installing, repairing, rebuilding, and adjusting mechanical or electronic locking devices, safes, and security vaults.

This industry group provides services to buildings and dwellings, e.g., exterminating/pest control, janitorial services (interior cleaning), landscaping, carpet/upholstery cleaning, and other services like exterior cleaning, chimney cleaning, and pool maintenance.
This industry exterminates and controls birds, mosquitoes, rodents, termites, and other insects/pests (excluding for crop/forestry production). It includes fumigation services.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in exterminating and controlling birds, mosquitoes, rodents, termites, and other insects and pests (except for crop production and forestry production). Establishments providing fumigation services are included in this industry.

This industry cleans building interiors, interiors of transportation equipment (e.g., aircraft, rail cars, ships), and/or windows. It includes custodial, housekeeping, maid, and washroom sanitation services.
This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in cleaning building interiors, interiors of transportation equipment (e.g., aircraft, rail cars, ships), and/or windows.

This industry provides landscape care/maintenance services and/or installs trees, shrubs, plants, lawns, or gardens. It may also include designing landscape plans and constructing features like walkways, retaining walls, decks, and fences.

This industry comprises (1) establishments primarily engaged in providing landscape care and maintenance services and/or installing trees, shrubs, plants, lawns, or gardens and (2) establishments primarily engaged in providing these services along with the design of landscape plans and/or the construction (i.e., installation) of walkways, retaining walls, decks, fences, ponds, and similar structures.
This industry cleans and dyes used rugs, carpets, and upholstery.
This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in cleaning and dyeing used rugs, carpets, and upholstery.

naics_name: Other Services to Buildings and Dwellings
naics_desc: This industry provides services to buildings and dwellings (excluding exterminating/pest control, janitorial, landscaping care, and carpet/upholstery cleaning). Examples include building exterior cleaning (excluding sandblasting, window cleaning), swimming pool cleaning, chimney cleaning, and drain/gutter cleaning.

naics_name: Other Services to Buildings and Dwellings
naics_desc: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing services to buildings and dwellings (except exterminating and pest control; janitorial; landscaping care and maintenance; and carpet and upholstery cleaning).

This industry group provides day-to-day business and other organizational support services (excluding office administrative, facilities support, employment, business support, travel arrangement, security/investigation, and building services). It includes packaging, labeling, convention/trade show organizing, bartering, and traffic control.
This industry packages client-owned materials, including labeling and/or imprinting the package. Services include apparel/textile folding, blister packaging, and gift wrapping.
This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in packaging client-owned materials. The services may include labeling and/or imprinting the package.
This industry organizes, promotes, and/or manages events like business and trade shows, conventions, conferences, and meetings, whether or not they manage the facilities.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in organizing, promoting, and/or managing events, such as business and trade shows, conventions, conferences, and meetings (whether or not they manage and provide the staff to operate the facilities in which these events take place).

This industry provides day-to-day business and organizational support services (excluding office administrative, facilities support, employment, business support, travel arrangement, security/investigation, building services, packaging/labeling, and convention/trade show organizing). Examples include bartering, flagging (traffic control), bottle exchanges, inventory taking, and lumber grading services.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing day-to-day business and other organizational support services (except office administrative services, facilities support services, employment services, business support services, travel arrangement and reservation services, security and investigation services, services to buildings and other structures, packaging and labeling services, and convention and trade show organizing services).

This subsector collects, treats, and disposes of waste materials, including local hauling, operating recovery facilities for recyclables, remediation services (e.g., hazardous material cleanup), and septic pumping. Excludes long-distance waste hauling and municipal sewer systems.

This industry group collects and/or hauls hazardous, nonhazardous, and/or recyclable waste materials within a local area. It also operates waste transfer stations and may involve identification, treatment, packaging, and labeling of hazardous waste.

This industry collects and/or hauls hazardous, nonhazardous, and/or recyclable waste materials within a local area. It also operates waste transfer stations and may identify, treat, package, and label waste for transport.

This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in one or more of the following: (1) collecting and/or hauling nonhazardous solid waste (i.e., garbage) within a local area; (2) operating nonhazardous solid waste transfer stations; and (3) collecting and/or hauling mixed recyclable materials within a local area.

This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in collecting and/or hauling hazardous waste within a local area and/or operating hazardous waste transfer stations. Hazardous waste collection establishments may be responsible for the identification, treatment, packaging, and labeling of waste for the purposes of transport.

This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in collecting and/or hauling waste (except nonhazardous solid waste and hazardous waste) within a local area. Establishments engaged in brush or rubble removal services are included in this industry.

This industry group operates waste treatment/disposal facilities (excluding sewer systems), or combines local collection/hauling with operating such facilities. It includes waste combustors/incinerators, solid waste landfills, and compost dumps.

This industry operates waste treatment/disposal facilities (excluding sewer systems) or combines local collection/hauling with operating such facilities. It includes waste combustors/incinerators, solid waste landfills, and compost dumps.

This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in (1) operating treatment and/or disposal facilities for hazardous waste or (2) the combined activity of collecting and/or hauling of hazardous waste materials within a local area and operating treatment or disposal facilities for hazardous waste.

This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in (1) operating landfills for the disposal of nonhazardous solid waste or (2) the combined activity of collecting and/or hauling nonhazardous waste materials within a local area and operating landfills for the disposal of nonhazardous solid waste. These establishments may produce byproducts, such as methane.

This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in operating combustors and incinerators for the disposal of nonhazardous solid waste. These establishments may produce byproducts, such as electricity and steam.

naics_name: Other Nonhazardous Waste Treatment and Disposal
naics_desc: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in (1) operating nonhazardous waste treatment and disposal facilities (except landfills, combustors, incinerators, and sewer systems or sewage treatment facilities) or (2) the combined activity of collecting and/or hauling of nonhazardous waste materials within a local area and operating waste treatment or disposal facilities (except landfills, combustors, incinerators, and sewer systems or sewage treatment facilities). Compost dumps are included in this industry.

naics_name: Remediation and Other Waste Management Services
naics_desc: This industry group provides remediation and other waste management services (excluding waste collection, treatment, disposal, and materials recovery). It includes cleanup of contaminated sites, integrated mine reclamation, asbestos/lead paint abatement, septic tank pumping, and sewer cleaning.

This industry remediates/cleans up contaminated buildings, mine sites, soil, or ground water. It includes integrated mine reclamation (demolition, soil remediation, wastewater treatment, hazardous material removal, contouring, revegetation) and asbestos, lead paint, and other toxic material abatement.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in one or more of the following: (1) remediation and cleanup of contaminated buildings, mine sites, soil, or ground water; (2) integrated mine reclamation activities, including demolition, soil remediation, waste water treatment, hazardous material removal, contouring land, and revegetation; and (3) asbestos, lead paint, and other toxic material abatement.

This industry operates facilities for separating/sorting recyclable materials from nonhazardous waste streams (garbage) and/or for sorting commingled recyclables (paper, plastics, used beverage cans, metals) into distinct categories.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in (1) operating facilities for separating and sorting recyclable materials from nonhazardous waste streams (i.e., garbage) and/or (2) operating facilities where commingled recyclable materials, such as paper, plastics, used beverage cans, and metals, are sorted into distinct categories.

This industry provides waste management services (excluding waste collection, treatment/disposal, remediation, materials recovery facilities, and waste management consulting services). It includes septic tank pumping, portable toilet rental/servicing, beach cleaning, and sewer/storm basin cleanout.
This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in (1) pumping (i.e., cleaning) septic tanks and cesspools and/or (2) renting and/or servicing portable toilets.

naics_name: All Other Miscellaneous Waste Management Services
naics_desc: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing waste management services (except waste collection, waste treatment and disposal, remediation, operation of materials recovery facilities, septic tank pumping and related services, and waste management consulting services).

This sector encompasses establishments providing instruction and training in diverse subjects, from schools and colleges to universities and training centers. Services may include academic, technical, and specialized instruction delivered in various settings.

This subsector provides instruction and training across diverse subjects, from elementary schools to universities and specialized training centers. The structure is based on educational level and type of service, with varying instructor training requirements.
This industry group furnishes academic courses and associated coursework for basic preparatory education (kindergarten through 12th grade), including school boards and districts.

This industry furnishes academic courses and associated coursework for basic preparatory education (kindergarten through 12th grade). It includes school boards, school districts, charter schools, and schools for physically disabled.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in furnishing academic courses and associated course work that comprise a basic preparatory education. A basic preparatory education ordinarily constitutes kindergarten through 12th grade. This industry includes school boards and school districts.

This industry group furnishes academic or academic and technical courses, granting associate degrees, certificates, or diplomas below the baccalaureate level. Admission requires at least a high school diploma or equivalent.

This industry furnishes academic or academic and technical courses, granting associate degrees, certificates, or diplomas below the baccalaureate level. Admission requires at least a high school diploma or equivalent.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in furnishing academic, or academic and technical, courses and granting associate degrees, certificates, or diplomas below the baccalaureate level. The requirement for admission to an associate or equivalent degree program is at least a high school diploma or equivalent general academic training. Instruction may be provided in diverse settings, such as the establishment's or client's training facilities, educational institutions, the workplace, or the home, and through diverse means, such as correspondence, television, the Internet, or other electronic and distance-learning methods. The training provided by these establishments may include the use of simulators and simulation methods.

This industry group furnishes academic courses and grants baccalaureate or graduate degrees. Admission requires at least a high school diploma or equivalent, with instruction offered in diverse settings and methods.

This industry furnishes academic courses and grants baccalaureate or graduate degrees. Admission requires at least a high school diploma or equivalent, with instruction offered in diverse settings and methods, e.g., military academies, theological seminaries.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in furnishing academic courses and granting degrees at baccalaureate or graduate levels. The requirement for admission is at least a high school diploma or equivalent general academic training. Instruction may be provided in diverse settings, such as the establishment's or client's training facilities, educational institutions, the workplace, or the home, and through diverse means, such as correspondence, television, the Internet, or other electronic and distance-learning methods. The training provided by these establishments may include the use of simulators and simulation methods.

naics_name: Business Schools and Computer and Management Training
naics_desc: This industry group offers courses in office procedures, secretarial/stenographic skills, computer training (excluding repair), and short-duration courses/seminars for management/professional development. Instruction is provided in diverse settings using various methods, including simulators.

This industry offers courses in office procedures, secretarial/stenographic skills, and basic office skills (e.g., word processing). It also provides classes in office machine operation, reception, communications, and other skills for clerical/secretarial careers.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in offering courses in office procedures and secretarial and stenographic skills and may offer courses in basic office skills, such as word processing. In addition, these establishments may offer such classes as office machine operation, reception, communications, and other skills designed for individuals pursuing a clerical or secretarial career. Instruction may be provided in diverse settings, such as the establishment's or client's training facilities, educational institutions, the workplace, or the home, and through diverse means, such as correspondence, television, the Internet, or other electronic and distance-learning methods. The training provided by these establishments may include the use of simulators and simulation methods.

This industry conducts computer training (excluding computer repair) in programming, software applications, computerized business systems, electronics technology, computer operations, and LAN management. Instruction is provided in diverse settings and methods, including simulators.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in conducting computer training (except computer repair), such as instruction in computer programming, software applications, computerized business systems, computer electronics technology, computer operations, and local area network management. Instruction may be provided in diverse settings, such as the establishment's or client's training facilities, educational institutions, the workplace, or the home, and through diverse means, such as correspondence, television, the Internet, or other electronic and distance-learning methods. The training provided by these establishments may include the use of simulators and simulation methods.

This industry offers short-duration courses and seminars for management and professional development, provided directly to individuals or through employer programs. Courses can be customized and utilize diverse instruction settings and methods, including simulators.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in offering an array of short duration courses and seminars for management and professional development. Training for career development may be provided directly to individuals or through employers' training programs, and courses may be customized or modified to meet the special needs of customers. Instruction may be provided in diverse settings, such as the establishment's or client's training facilities, educational institutions, the workplace, or the home, and through diverse means, such as correspondence, television, the Internet, or other electronic and distance-learning methods. The training provided by these establishments may include the use of simulators and simulation methods.

This industry group offers vocational and technical training in subjects and trades leading to job-specific certification. Instruction is structured and specialized, covering areas like cosmetology, aviation, and various trades, using diverse methods including simulators.

This industry offers job or career vocational/technical courses (excluding cosmetology, barber, aviation/flight, and apprenticeship training). Curriculums are highly structured, specialized, and lead to job-specific certification, e.g., bartending, broadcasting, computer repair, truck driving, graphic arts, and real estate.

This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in offering training in barbering, hair styling, or the cosmetic arts, such as makeup or skin care. These schools provide job-specific certification.
This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in offering aviation and flight training. These establishments may offer vocational training, recreational training, or both.
This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in offering apprenticeship training programs. These programs involve applied training as well as course work.

This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in offering job or career vocational or technical courses (except cosmetology and barber training, aviation and flight training, and apprenticeship training). The curriculums offered by these schools are highly structured and specialized and lead to job-specific certification.

This industry group offers diverse instruction (excluding academic, business, computer, management, technical/trade). It includes fine arts, sports, recreation, language, exam preparation, tutoring, and automobile driving instruction, provided in various settings and methods.

This industry offers instruction in the arts, e.g., dance, art, drama, and music, including dance studios, music schools, performing arts schools (excluding academic), and photography schools (excluding commercial).
This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in offering instruction in the arts, including dance, art, drama, and music.

This industry offers instruction in athletic activities, including camps and schools. It encompasses overnight/day sports instruction camps, professional sports instructors (non-participating), cheerleading, gymnastics, martial arts, riding, and swimming instruction.

This industry comprises establishments, such as camps and schools, primarily engaged in offering instruction in athletic activities. Overnight and day sports instruction camps are included in this industry.
This industry offers foreign language instruction (including sign language), from conversational skills for personal enrichment to intensive training courses for career or educational opportunities.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in offering foreign language instruction (including sign language). These establishments are designed to offer language instruction ranging from conversational skills for personal enrichment to intensive training courses for career or educational opportunities.

This industry offers instruction (excluding academic schools, colleges, universities, business/computer/management, technical/trade, fine arts, sports, recreation, language, exam preparation, tutoring, and automobile driving instruction). Examples include public speaking training, survival training, and speed reading instruction.
This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in offering preparation for standardized examinations and/or academic tutoring services.
This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in offering automobile driving instruction.

naics_name: All Other Miscellaneous Schools and Instruction
naics_desc: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in offering instruction (except academic schools, colleges, and universities; business, computer, and management instruction; technical and trade instruction; and fine arts, sports, recreation, language, exam preparation, tutoring, and automobile driving instruction).

This industry group provides non-instructional services supporting educational processes or systems, e.g., educational consultants, testing services, guidance counseling, and student exchange programs.
This industry provides non-instructional services supporting educational processes or systems, e.g., educational consultants, testing services, guidance counseling, and student exchange programs.
This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing non-instructional services that support educational processes or systems.

This sector delivers health care and social assistance services to individuals, ranging from medical care (e.g., hospitals, outpatient clinics) to social support (e.g., family services, community housing). Services are provided by trained professionals, often with a mix of health and social service components.

This subsector delivers direct or indirect health care services to ambulatory patients, typically not providing inpatient services. Health practitioners in this area focus on outpatient services, where facilities and equipment are not the most significant part of the production process.

This industry group includes offices of licensed physicians (M.D. or D.O.) practicing general or specialized medicine/surgery independently, in their own offices or facilities of others. It distinguishes between mental health specialists and all other physicians.

This industry comprises establishments of health practitioners having the degree of M.D. (Doctor of Medicine) or D.O. (Doctor of Osteopathic Medicine) primarily engaged in the independent practice of general or specialized medicine (except psychiatry or psychoanalysis) or surgery.  These practitioners operate private or group practices in their own offices (e.g., centers, clinics) or in the facilities of others, such as hospitals or HMO medical centers.

naics_name: Offices of Physicians (except Mental Health Specialists)
naics_desc: This industry comprises establishments of health practitioners having the degree of M.D. (Doctor of Medicine) or D.O. (Doctor of Osteopathic Medicine) primarily engaged in the independent practice of general or specialized medicine (excluding psychiatry or psychoanalysis) or surgery. These practitioners operate private/group practices in their own offices or facilities of others.

naics_name: Offices of Physicians, Mental Health Specialists
naics_desc: This industry comprises establishments of health practitioners (M.D. or D.O.) primarily engaged in the independent practice of psychiatry or psychoanalysis. These practitioners operate private/group practices in their own offices or facilities of others.

This industry group includes offices of licensed dentists (D.M.D., D.D.S., or D.D.Sc.) practicing general or specialized dentistry/dental surgery independently, in their own offices or facilities of others. Services range from preventive to emergency care.
See industry description for 621210.

This industry comprises establishments of licensed dentists (D.M.D., D.D.S., or D.D.Sc.) primarily practicing general or specialized dentistry/dental surgery independently, in their own offices or facilities of others. They provide comprehensive preventive, cosmetic, or emergency care, or specialize in a single field.

This industry group comprises offices of independent health practitioners (excluding physicians and dentists). It includes chiropractors, optometrists, mental health specialists, physical/occupational/speech therapists, audiologists, podiatrists, acupuncturists, and other miscellaneous health practitioners.
See industry description for 621310.

This industry comprises establishments of licensed chiropractors (D.C.) primarily practicing chiropractic, providing diagnostic/therapeutic treatment of neuromusculoskeletal disorders through spinal/extremity manipulation. They operate private/group practices in their own offices or facilities of others.
See industry description for 621320.

This industry comprises establishments of licensed optometrists (O.D.) primarily practicing optometry, examining, diagnosing, treating, and managing visual system disorders. They prescribe/provide eyeglasses, contact lenses, low vision aids, and vision therapy, and may offer optician services.

naics_name: Offices of Mental Health Practitioners (except Physicians)
naics_desc: See industry description for 621330.

naics_name: Offices of Mental Health Practitioners (except Physicians)
naics_desc: This industry comprises establishments of independent mental health practitioners (excluding physicians) primarily diagnosing/treating mental/emotional/behavioral disorders and/or individual/group social dysfunction. They operate private/group practices in their own offices or facilities of others.

naics_name: Offices of Physical, Occupational and Speech Therapists, and Audiologists
naics_desc: See industry description for 621340.

naics_name: Offices of Physical, Occupational and Speech Therapists, and Audiologists
naics_desc: This industry comprises establishments of independent health practitioners providing physical therapy, occupational therapy (helping regain physical/mental functioning), and speech/language/hearing diagnosis/treatment. They operate private/group practices in their own offices or facilities of others.

naics_name: Offices of All Other Health Practitioners
naics_desc: This industry comprises establishments of independent health practitioners (except physicians; dentists; chiropractors; optometrists; mental health specialists; physical, occupational, and speech therapists; and audiologists). These practitioners operate private or group practices in their own offices (e.g., centers, clinics) or in the facilities of others, such as hospitals or HMO medical centers.

This industry comprises establishments of licensed podiatrists (D.P.M.) primarily practicing podiatry, diagnosing and treating diseases/deformities of the foot. They operate private/group practices in their own offices or facilities of others.

naics_name: Offices of All Other Miscellaneous Health Practitioners
naics_desc: This industry comprises establishments of independent health practitioners (excluding physicians, dentists, chiropractors, optometrists, mental health specialists, physical/occupational/speech therapists, audiologists, and podiatrists). They operate private/group practices in their own offices or facilities of others.

This industry group includes establishments with medical staff providing a range of outpatient services. It covers family planning, mental health/substance abuse treatment, HMO medical centers, kidney dialysis centers, freestanding ambulatory surgical/emergency centers, and other general or specialized outpatient care.
See industry description for 621410.

This industry provides a range of outpatient family planning services, e.g., contraceptive services, genetic/prenatal counseling, voluntary sterilization, and therapeutic/medically induced termination of pregnancy.

naics_name: Outpatient Mental Health and Substance Abuse Centers
naics_desc: See industry description for 621420.

naics_name: Outpatient Mental Health and Substance Abuse Centers
naics_desc: This industry provides outpatient services for diagnosing/treating mental health disorders and alcohol/substance abuse, typically for patients not requiring inpatient treatment. They offer counseling and information, or refer to more extensive programs.

This industry comprises establishments with medical staff primarily engaged in providing general or specialized outpatient care (except family planning centers and outpatient mental health and substance abuse centers). Centers or clinics of health practitioners with different degrees from more than one industry practicing within the same establishment (e.g., Doctor of Medicine and Doctor of Dental Medicine) are included in this industry.

This industry comprises HMO-owned establishments with physicians/medical staff primarily providing outpatient medical services to HMO subscribers, focusing on primary health care. It includes HMOs that both provide health care and underwrite health/medical insurance policies.
This industry comprises establishments with medical staff primarily providing outpatient kidney or renal dialysis services.

naics_name: Freestanding Ambulatory Surgical and Emergency Centers
naics_desc: This industry provides outpatient surgical services (e.g., orthoscopic, cataract surgery) or emergency care (e.g., setting broken bones, treating lacerations) on an outpatient basis. Establishments have specialized facilities and equipment like operating/recovery rooms and anesthetic/X-ray equipment.

This industry provides general or specialized outpatient care (excluding family planning, mental health/substance abuse, HMO medical centers, kidney dialysis centers, and freestanding surgical/emergency centers). It includes centers/clinics of health practitioners with different degrees practicing within the same establishment.

This industry group, known as medical and diagnostic laboratories, provides analytic or diagnostic services, including body fluid analysis and diagnostic imaging, generally on referral from a health practitioner.

This industry comprises establishments known as medical and diagnostic laboratories primarily engaged in providing analytic or diagnostic services, including body fluid analysis and diagnostic imaging, generally to the medical profession or to the patient on referral from a health practitioner.

This industry, known as medical laboratories, provides analytic or diagnostic services, including body fluid analysis, generally to the medical profession or on referral from a health practitioner. Examples include blood analysis, medical pathology, and forensic laboratories.

This industry, known as diagnostic imaging centers, produces images of patients, generally on referral from a health practitioner. Examples include CT-scan, medical radiological, dental/medical X-ray, ultrasound, and MRI centers.

This industry group provides skilled nursing services in the home, along with a range of personal care, homemaker, physical therapy, medical social, and high-tech care services. It includes home health care agencies, visiting nurse associations, and in-home hospice care.
See industry description for 621610.

This industry provides skilled nursing services in the home, along with a range of personal care, homemaker, physical therapy, medical social services, medications, medical equipment, counseling, and high-tech care like intravenous therapy.

This industry group provides ambulatory health care services (excluding offices of practitioners, outpatient centers, laboratories, home health care, and ambulances). It includes blood/organ banks, health screening, and pacemaker monitoring services.
See industry description for 621910.

This industry transports patients by ground or air, along with medical care. Services are often provided during medical emergencies but not restricted to them, utilizing vehicles equipped with lifesaving equipment operated by medically trained personnel.

naics_name: All Other Ambulatory Health Care Services
naics_desc: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing ambulatory health care services (except offices of physicians, dentists, and other health practitioners; outpatient care centers; medical and diagnostic laboratories; home health care providers; and ambulances).
This industry collects, stores, and distributes blood and blood products, and stores and distributes body organs.

naics_name: All Other Miscellaneous Ambulatory Health Care Services
naics_desc: This industry provides ambulatory health care services (excluding offices of physicians, dentists, other health practitioners, outpatient care centers, medical/diagnostic laboratories, home health care, ambulances, blood/organ banks). Examples include health screening, hearing testing, pacemaker monitoring, and smoking cessation programs.

This subsector provides medical, diagnostic, and treatment services, including physician, nursing, and other health services to inpatients. Hospitals offer specialized accommodation and rely on facilities and equipment as integral parts of their production process.

This industry group operates licensed general medical and surgical hospitals, providing diagnostic and medical treatment (surgical and nonsurgical) to inpatients with diverse conditions. Services include outpatient care, laboratory, X-ray, and pharmacy.
See industry description for 622110.

This industry operates licensed general medical/surgical hospitals, providing diagnostic and medical treatment (surgical/nonsurgical) to inpatients with diverse conditions. Hospitals maintain beds, provide food, and have an organized staff of physicians/medical staff, usually offering outpatient, laboratory, X-ray, and pharmacy services.

This industry group operates licensed psychiatric and substance abuse hospitals, providing diagnostic, medical treatment, and monitoring services for inpatients with mental illness or substance abuse disorders. Services include psychiatric, psychological, and social work.
See industry description for 622210.

This industry operates licensed psychiatric and substance abuse hospitals, providing diagnostic, medical treatment, and monitoring for inpatients with mental illness or substance abuse disorders, often requiring extended stays. Services include psychiatric, psychological, and social work, with typical outpatient, laboratory, and X-ray services.

naics_name: Specialty (except Psychiatric and Substance Abuse) Hospitals
naics_desc: This industry group operates licensed specialty hospitals (excluding psychiatric/substance abuse), providing diagnostic and medical treatment to inpatients with specific diseases or conditions. It includes long-term care, rehabilitation, and restorative services.

naics_name: Specialty (except Psychiatric and Substance Abuse) Hospitals
naics_desc: See industry description for 622310.

naics_name: Specialty (except Psychiatric and Substance Abuse) Hospitals
naics_desc: This industry operates licensed specialty hospitals (excluding psychiatric/substance abuse), providing diagnostic and medical treatment to inpatients with specific diseases/conditions. It includes long-term care for the chronically ill and rehabilitation services for physically challenged individuals, offering various support services.

This subsector provides residential care combined with nursing, supervisory, or other care as needed by residents. Facilities are a significant part of the production process, offering a mix of health and social services, often with substantial nursing components.

This industry group provides inpatient nursing and rehabilitative services for extended periods to individuals requiring continuous nursing care. Establishments have a permanent staff of registered or licensed practical nurses, e.g., convalescent homes, nursing homes, inpatient hospices.
See industry description for 623110.

This industry provides inpatient nursing and rehabilitative services for extended periods to individuals requiring nursing care. Establishments have a permanent core staff of registered/licensed practical nurses and other staff for continuous personal care, e.g., convalescent homes and inpatient hospices.

naics_name: Residential Intellectual and Developmental Disability, Mental Health, and Substance Abuse Facilities
naics_desc: This industry group provides residential care for persons with intellectual/developmental disabilities, mental health, or substance abuse problems. Facilities offer room, board, supervision, and counseling, with medical services being incidental to mental rehabilitation and support.

naics_name: Residential Intellectual and Developmental Disability Facilities
naics_desc: See industry description for 623210.

naics_name: Residential Intellectual and Developmental Disability Facilities
naics_desc: This industry (e.g., group homes, hospitals, intermediate care facilities) provides residential care services for persons with intellectual and developmental disabilities. Facilities offer room, board, protective supervision, and counseling, with any health care being incidental.

naics_name: Residential Mental Health and Substance Abuse Facilities
naics_desc: See industry description for 623220.

naics_name: Residential Mental Health and Substance Abuse Facilities
naics_desc: This industry provides residential care and treatment for patients with mental health and substance abuse illnesses, offering room, board, supervision, and counseling services. Medical services are incidental to the counseling, mental rehabilitation, and support services.

naics_name: Continuing Care Retirement Communities and Assisted Living Facilities for the Elderly
naics_desc: This industry group provides residential and personal care services for the elderly or persons unable/unwilling to live independently. Services include room, board, supervision, and daily living assistance, with or without on-site nursing care.

naics_name: Continuing Care Retirement Communities and Assisted Living Facilities for the Elderly
naics_desc: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing residential and personal care services for (1) the elderly and other persons who are unable to fully care for themselves and/or (2) the elderly and other persons who do not desire to live independently. The care typically includes room, board, supervision, and assistance in daily living, such as housekeeping services. In some instances, these establishments provide skilled nursing care for residents in separate on-site facilities.

This industry provides residential and personal care services with on-site nursing care facilities for the elderly or persons unable/unwilling to live independently. Residents live in various settings with meals, housekeeping, social, leisure, and other daily living assistance services.

naics_name: Assisted Living Facilities for the Elderly
naics_desc: This industry provides residential and personal care services (without nursing care) for the elderly or persons unable/unwilling to live independently. Care typically includes room, board, supervision, and assistance in daily living, e.g., housekeeping.

This industry group provides residential care (excluding intellectual/developmental disability, mental health/substance abuse, continuing care retirement, and assisted living facilities for the elderly). It offers supervision and personal care services, e.g., boot camps for youth, group homes for disabled, or orphanages.
See industry description for 623990.

This industry provides residential care (excluding intellectual/developmental disability, mental health/substance abuse, continuing care retirement, and assisted living facilities for the elderly). It also provides supervision and personal care services, e.g., boot/disciplinary camps for delinquent youth, group homes for disabled, and orphanages.

This subsector provides a wide variety of direct social assistance services to clients, excluding residential or accommodation services, except for short stays. These services encompass areas like child/youth support, elderly/disabled services, food/housing aid, and vocational rehabilitation.

This industry group provides nonresidential social assistance services to children, youth, the elderly, persons with disabilities, and other individuals/families. Services include adoption, foster care, drug prevention, life skills, day care, non-medical home care, crisis intervention, and counseling.
See industry description for 624110.

This industry provides nonresidential social assistance services for children and youth, focusing on adoption, foster care, drug prevention, life skills training, and positive social development, e.g., child guidance organizations and youth centers (excluding recreational only).

naics_name: Services for the Elderly and Persons with Disabilities
naics_desc: See industry description for 624120.

naics_name: Services for the Elderly and Persons with Disabilities
naics_desc: This industry provides nonresidential social assistance services to improve quality of life for the elderly or persons with intellectual/developmental disabilities. Services include day care, non-medical home care/homemaker services, social activities, group support, and companionship.
See industry description for 624190.

This industry provides nonresidential individual and family social assistance services (excluding children, elderly, persons with disabilities). Examples include community action services, crisis intervention, family social services, hotline centers, marriage counseling, and telephone counseling.

naics_name: Community Food and Housing, and Emergency and Other Relief Services
naics_desc: This industry group collects, prepares, and delivers food for the needy; provides short-term emergency/temporary residential shelter; offers transitional housing; assists in volunteer construction/repair of low-cost housing; and provides relief services (food, shelter, clothing, medical) to disaster/conflict victims.
See industry description for 624210.

This industry collects, prepares, and delivers food for the needy. Establishments may distribute clothing/blankets, prepare/deliver meals to the ill/disabled, collect/distribute donated food, or provide meals at fixed/mobile locations, e.g., food banks, soup kitchens.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing one or more of the following community housing services: (1) short-term emergency shelter for victims of domestic violence, sexual assault, or child abuse; (2) temporary residential shelter for the homeless, runaway youths, and patients and families caught in medical crises; (3) transitional housing for low-income individuals and families; (4) volunteer construction or repair of low-cost housing, in partnership with the homeowner who may assist in construction or repair work; and (5) repair of homes for elderly or disabled homeowners. These establishments may operate their own shelter, they may subsidize housing using existing homes, apartments, hotels, or motels, or they may require a low-cost mortgage or work (sweat) equity.

This industry provides short-term emergency shelter for victims of domestic violence, sexual assault, or child abuse, and/or temporary residential shelter for homeless individuals/families, runaway youth, and patients/families in medical crises. Shelters may be operated directly or housing subsidized.

This industry provides community housing services (excluding temporary shelters), e.g., transitional housing to low-income individuals, volunteer construction/repair of low-cost housing, and home repair for elderly/disabled homeowners. Establishments may subsidize housing or provide furniture/household supplies.
See industry description for 624230.
This industry provides food, shelter, clothing, medical relief, resettlement, and counseling to victims of domestic or international disasters or conflicts (e.g., wars).

This industry group provides vocational rehabilitation or habilitation services, e.g., job counseling, training, and work experience, to unemployed, underemployed, and disabled persons. It also trains and employs persons with disabilities, including sheltered workshops.
See industry description for 624310.

This industry provides vocational rehabilitation or habilitation services, e.g., job counseling, training, and work experience, to unemployed, underemployed, and disabled persons. It also provides training and employment to persons with disabilities, including sheltered workshops.

This industry group provides care and early learning for infants and children (birth through school age). Establishments may offer pre-kindergarten, kindergarten, or before/after-school educational programs, focusing on health, social, emotional learning, and family engagement.
See industry description for 624410.

This industry provides care and early learning opportunities for infants and children (birth through school age). It may offer pre-kindergarten, kindergarten, and/or before/after-school educational programs, focusing on health, social/emotional learning, and family engagement.

This sector provides cultural, entertainment, and recreational services. Activities include live performances (theater, music, sports), preserving/exhibiting historical sites and museums, and operating facilities for recreational activities like amusement parks, gambling, and sports centers.

naics_name: Performing Arts, Spectator Sports, and Related Industries
naics_desc: This subsector produces or organizes live performances, events, or exhibits, involving actors, singers, dancers, musicians, athletes, and other entertainers. It distinguishes producing events, organizing/promoting, managing entertainers, and providing artistic/technical skills for live events.
This industry group produces live theatrical presentations, e.g., musicals, operas, plays, comedy, dance, mime, puppet shows. Companies may or may not operate their own venues.
See industry description for 711110.

This industry comprises companies/groups/theaters producing live theatrical presentations (musicals, operas, plays, comedy, mime, puppet shows). It also includes dinner theaters producing live shows and providing food/beverages.
See industry description for 711120.

This industry comprises companies/groups/theaters producing all types of live theatrical dance presentations (e.g., ballet, contemporary dance, folk dance). Dance companies may or may not operate their own venues.
See industry description for 711130.

This industry produces live musical entertainment (excluding theatrical musical/opera productions) and includes independent (freelance) artists providing live musical entertainment. Groups/artists may perform live or in a studio, and may or may not operate their own facilities.
See industry description for 711190.

This industry comprises companies/groups (excluding theater companies, dance companies, musical groups/artists) primarily producing live theatrical presentations, e.g., carnival traveling shows, circuses, ice skating companies, and magic shows.

This industry group involves professional/semiprofessional sports teams/clubs participating in live events for paying audiences, operating racetracks without casinos, or independent athletes/owners participating in races. It also includes establishments supporting sports event participants.

This industry comprises (1) sports teams or clubs primarily participating in live sporting events before a paying audience; (2) establishments primarily engaged in operating racetracks without casinos; (3) independent athletes engaged in participating in live sporting or racing events before a paying audience; (4) owners of racing participants, such as cars, dogs, and horses, primarily engaged in entering them in racing events or other spectator sports events; and (5) establishments, such as sports trainers, primarily engaged in providing specialized services to support participants in sports events or competitions. The sports teams and clubs included in this industry may or may not operate their own arena, stadium, or other facility for presenting their games or other spectator sports events.

This industry comprises professional or semiprofessional sports teams/clubs participating in live sporting events (e.g., baseball, basketball, football, hockey, soccer) before a paying audience. They may or may not operate their own arenas or stadiums.
This industry operates racetracks without casinos, and may also present/promote events like auto, dog, and horse races held in these facilities.

This industry includes independent athletes (golfers, boxers, race car drivers) participating in live sporting/racing events for a paying audience; owners of racing participants (cars, dogs, horses) entering them in events; and sports trainers providing specialized support services for competitors.

naics_name: Promoters of Performing Arts, Sports, and Similar Events
naics_desc: This industry group organizes, promotes, and/or manages live performing arts productions, sports events, and similar events (e.g., fairs, concerts, festivals). Promoters may manage/operate the facilities or use facilities managed by others.

naics_name: Promoters of Performing Arts, Sports, and Similar Events with Facilities
naics_desc: See industry description for 711310.

naics_name: Promoters of Performing Arts, Sports, and Similar Events with Facilities
naics_desc: This industry organizes, promotes, and/or manages live performing arts productions, sports events, and similar events (e.g., fairs, concerts, festivals) held in facilities they manage and operate. They may also manage/staff arenas, stadiums, or theaters for rent to other promoters.

naics_name: Promoters of Performing Arts, Sports, and Similar Events without Facilities
naics_desc: See industry description for 711320.

naics_name: Promoters of Performing Arts, Sports, and Similar Events without Facilities
naics_desc: This industry organizes, promotes, and/or manages live performing arts productions, sports events, and similar events (e.g., fairs, concerts, festivals) in facilities managed and operated by others. Theatrical (excluding motion picture) booking agencies are included.

naics_name: Agents and Managers for Artists, Athletes, Entertainers, and Other Public Figures
naics_desc: This industry group represents and/or manages creative/performing artists, sports figures, entertainers, and other public figures. Services include contract negotiations, financial management, and career promotion.

naics_name: Agents and Managers for Artists, Athletes, Entertainers, and Other Public Figures
naics_desc: See industry description for 711410.

naics_name: Agents and Managers for Artists, Athletes, Entertainers, and Other Public Figures
naics_desc: This industry represents and/or manages creative/performing artists, sports figures, entertainers, and other public figures. Services include contract negotiations, financial affairs management, and career promotion.

This industry group consists of independent (freelance) individuals performing in artistic productions, creating artistic/cultural works, or providing technical expertise for these productions. It also includes athletes/celebrities endorsing products or making public appearances for fees.
See industry description for 711510.

This industry comprises independent (freelance) individuals performing in artistic productions, creating artistic/cultural works, or providing technical expertise for these productions. It also includes athletes/celebrities endorsing products or making speeches/public appearances for a fee.

naics_name: Museums, Historical Sites, and Similar Institutions
naics_desc: This subsector preserves and exhibits objects, sites, and natural wonders of historical, cultural, or educational value, like museums, historical sites, zoos, botanical gardens, and nature parks.

naics_name: Museums, Historical Sites, and Similar Institutions
naics_desc: This industry group preserves and exhibits objects, sites, and natural wonders of historical, cultural, and/or educational value. It includes art/science museums, historical sites, zoos, botanical gardens, and nature parks.
See industry description for 712110.

This industry preserves and exhibits objects of historical, cultural, and/or educational value, e.g., art galleries (excluding retail), art museums, halls of fame, planetariums, science/technology museums, and wax museums.
See industry description for 712120.

This industry preserves and exhibits sites, buildings, forts, or communities describing events/persons of historical interest. It includes archeological sites, battlefields, historical ships, and pioneer villages.
See industry description for 712130.
This industry preserves and exhibits live plant and animal life displays, e.g., aquariums, arboreta, aviaries, wild animal parks, and zoological gardens.

naics_name: Nature Parks and Other Similar Institutions
naics_desc: See industry description for 712190.

naics_name: Nature Parks and Other Similar Institutions
naics_desc: This industry preserves and exhibits natural areas or settings, e.g., bird/wildlife sanctuaries, conservation areas, national parks, nature centers, and natural wonder tourist attractions (caverns, waterfalls).

This subsector operates facilities or provides services for sports, recreation, amusement, or gambling. It includes amusement parks, arcades, gambling establishments, golf courses, skiing facilities, marinas, fitness centers, and bowling centers.
This industry group operates amusement or theme parks with various attractions (rides, games, shows) and amusement arcades/parlors (excluding gambling, billiard, or pool).
See industry description for 713110.

This industry operates amusement or theme parks with various attractions, e.g., mechanical rides, water rides, games, shows, theme exhibits, refreshment stands, and picnic grounds. They may lease concession space.
See industry description for 713120.
This industry operates amusement (excluding gambling, billiard, or pool) arcades and parlors.

This industry group operates gambling facilities (excluding casino hotels) or provides gambling services. It includes casinos (without hotels), bingo halls, video gaming terminals, bookmakers, lotteries, and card rooms.
See industry description for 713210.

This industry operates gambling facilities (excluding casino hotels) offering table wagering games and other gambling activities like slot machines and sports betting. They often provide food and beverage services, and include floating casinos and casinos with racetracks.
See industry description for 713290.

This industry operates gambling facilities (excluding casinos/casino hotels) or provides gambling services. It includes bingo, off-track betting, slot machine parlors, bookmakers, lottery ticket sales agents (excluding retail stores), and card rooms.
