This industry group provides recreational and amusement services (excluding amusement parks/arcades, gambling industries, golf courses/country clubs, skiing facilities, marinas, fitness centers, and bowling centers). It includes miniature golf, driving ranges, shooting ranges, recreational camps, and escape rooms.
See industry description for 713910.

This industry operates golf courses (excluding miniature) and/or country clubs with golf courses, dining, and other recreational facilities. They often provide food/beverage services, equipment rental, and golf instruction.
See industry description for 713920.

This industry operates downhill, cross-country, or related skiing areas and/or ski lifts/tows. They often provide food/beverage services, equipment rental, and ski instruction. Four-season resorts without accommodations are included.
See industry description for 713930.

This industry operates docking and/or storage facilities for pleasure craft owners, optionally with related activities like retailing fuel/marine supplies, and repairing, maintaining, or renting pleasure boats.
See industry description for 713940.

This industry operates fitness and recreational sports facilities for exercise and active physical fitness conditioning or recreational sports, e.g., swimming, skating, racquet sports. Examples include aerobic dance centers, gymnasiums, and handball clubs.
See industry description for 713950.
This industry operates bowling centers, often providing food and beverage services.

naics_name: All Other Amusement and Recreation Industries
naics_desc: See industry description for 713990.

naics_name: All Other Amusement and Recreation Industries
naics_desc: This industry provides recreational and amusement services (excluding amusement parks/arcades, gambling, golf courses/country clubs, skiing, marinas, fitness centers, bowling centers). It includes amusement ride/nongambling device concession operators, miniature golf, driving ranges, shooting ranges, recreational camps, billiard parlors, and escape rooms.

This sector provides lodging and/or prepares meals, snacks, and beverages for immediate consumption. It includes hotels, motels, recreational camps, rooming houses, and various food service establishments like restaurants, caterers, and mobile food vendors.

This subsector provides lodging or short-term accommodations for travelers and others. It includes hotels, motels, resort hotels, bed-and-breakfast inns, RV parks, campgrounds, and longer-term facilities like rooming houses and workers' camps.

This industry group provides short-term lodging in hotels, motels, resort hotels, casino hotels, and bed-and-breakfast inns. Establishments may offer additional services like food, recreation, and conference facilities.
See industry description for 721110.

This industry provides short-term lodging in hotels, motor hotels, resort hotels, and motels (excluding casino hotels). Establishments may offer food/beverage, recreational services, conference rooms, laundry, and parking.
See industry description for 721120.

This industry provides short-term lodging in hotel facilities with an on-premises casino (table wagering, slot machines, sports betting). These establishments generally offer a range of services and amenities like food/beverage, entertainment, and conference facilities.
This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing short-term lodging (except hotels, motels, and casino hotels).

This industry provides short-term lodging in private homes or small converted buildings known as bed-and-breakfast inns. Services feature highly personalized service and include a full breakfast in the room rate.

This industry provides short-term lodging (excluding hotels, motels, casino hotels, and bed-and-breakfast inns). Examples include guest houses, tourist homes, housekeeping cabins/cottages, and hostels.

naics_name: RV (Recreational Vehicle) Parks and Recreational Camps
naics_desc: This industry group operates RV parks, campgrounds, and recreational/vacation camps (excluding instructional, day). These facilities accommodate campers, offer accommodation (cabins, fixed campsites), and provide amenities like food services and recreational activities.

naics_name: RV (Recreational Vehicle) Parks and Recreational Camps
naics_desc: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in operating recreational vehicle parks and campgrounds and recreational and vacation camps. These establishments cater to outdoor enthusiasts and are characterized by the type of accommodation and by the nature and the range of recreational facilities and activities provided to their clients.

This industry operates sites accommodating campers and their equipment (tents, tent trailers, travel trailers, RVs). Establishments may provide access to facilities like washrooms, laundry rooms, recreation halls, playgrounds, stores, and snack bars.

This industry operates overnight recreational camps (e.g., children's camps, family vacation camps, hunting/fishing camps, outdoor adventure retreats) offering activities like trail riding, white water rafting, hiking. Facilities provide accommodation (cabins, fixed campsites), food, and organized recreational activities.

naics_name: Rooming and Boarding Houses, Dormitories, and Workers' Camps
naics_desc: This industry group operates rooming and boarding houses, dormitories, and similar facilities like fraternity/sorority houses and workers' camps. These establishments provide temporary or longer-term accommodations that may serve as a principal residence, optionally with housekeeping, meals, and laundry.

naics_name: Rooming and Boarding Houses, Dormitories, and Workers' Camps
naics_desc: See industry description for 721310.

naics_name: Rooming and Boarding Houses, Dormitories, and Workers' Camps
naics_desc: This industry operates rooming/boarding houses and similar facilities (e.g., fraternity/sorority houses, off-campus dormitories, workers' camps). These establishments provide temporary or longer-term accommodations that may serve as a principal residence, optionally with housekeeping, meals, and laundry services.

This subsector prepares meals, snacks, and beverages for immediate consumption, on-premises or off-premises. Industries are grouped by service type, including food service contractors, caterers, mobile food services, drinking places, and various restaurants.

[Retired] Industries in the Food Services and Drinking Places subsector prepare meals, snacks, and beverages to customer order for immediate on-premises and off-premises consumption. There is a wide range of establishments in these industries. Some provide food and drink only, while others provide various combinations of seating space, waiter/waitress services, and incidental amenities, such as limited entertainment. The industries in the subsector are grouped based on the type and level of services provided. The industry groups are Special Food Services, such as food service contractors, caterers, and mobile food services; Drinking Places (Alcoholic Beverages); and Restaurants and Other Eating Places. Food and beverage services at hotels and motels, amusement parks, theaters, casinos, country clubs, similar recreational facilities, and civic and social organizations are included in this subsector only if these services are provided by a separate establishment primarily engaged in providing food and beverage services. Excluded from this subsector are establishments operating dinner cruises. These establishments are classified in Subsector 487, Scenic and Sightseeing Transportation, because they utilize transportation equipment to provide scenic recreational entertainment.

[Retired] Industries in the Food Services and Drinking Places subsector prepare meals, snacks, and beverages to customer order for immediate on-premises and off-premises consumption. There is a wide range of establishments in these industries. Some provide food and drink only, while others provide various combinations of seating space, waiter/waitress services, and incidental amenities, such as limited entertainment. These establishments are classified in Subsector 487, Scenic and Sightseeing Transportation, because they utilize transportation equipment to provide scenic recreational entertainment.

Excluded from this subsector are establishments operating dinner cruises. These establishments are classified in Subsector 487, Scenic and Sightseeing Transportation, because they utilize transportation equipment to provide scenic recreational entertainment.

Excluded from this subsector are establishments operating dinner cruises. These establishments are classified in Subsector 487, Scenic and Sightseeing Transportation, because they utilize transportation equipment to provide scenic recreational entertainment.

Excluded from this subsector are establishments operating dinner cruises. These establishments are classified in Subsector 487, Scenic and Sightseeing Transportation, because they utilize transportation equipment to provide scenic recreational entertainment.

There is a wide range of establishments in these industries. Some provide food and drink only, while others provide various combinations of seating space, waiter/waitress services, and incidental amenities, such as limited entertainment. Excluded from this subsector are establishments operating dinner cruises. These establishments are classified in Subsector 487, Scenic and Sightseeing Transportation, because they utilize transportation equipment to provide scenic recreational entertainment.

[Retired] Industries in the Food Services and Drinking Places subsector prepare meals, snacks, and beverages to customer order for immediate on-premises and off-premises consumption. There is a wide range of establishments in these industries. Some provide food and drink only, while others provide various combinations of seating space, waiter/waitress services, and incidental amenities, such as limited entertainment. The industries in the subsector are grouped based on the type and level of services provided. The industry groups are Special Food Services, such as food service contractors, caterers, and mobile food services; Drinking Places (Alcoholic Beverages); and Restaurants and Other Eating Places. Food and beverage services at hotels and motels, amusement parks, theaters, casinos, country clubs, similar recreational facilities, and civic and social organizations are included in this subsector only if these services are provided by a separate establishment primarily engaged in providing food and beverage services. Excluded from this subsector are establishments operating dinner cruises. These establishments are classified in Subsector 487, Scenic and Sightseeing Transportation, because they utilize transportation equipment to provide scenic recreational entertainment.

This industry group provides food services at customer locations, designated locations, or from motorized/nonmotorized vehicles. It includes food service contractors (e.g., airline, cafeteria, concession) and caterers for single event-based food services.
See industry description for 722310.

This industry provides food services at institutional, governmental, commercial, or industrial locations based on contractual arrangements. Services are for the convenience of the contracting organization/customers, with management staff always provided by the contractor.
See industry description for 722320.

This industry provides single event-based food services, typically transporting meals/snacks to events and/or preparing food off-premise. Banquet halls with catering staff are included, catering events like graduation parties, wedding receptions, and trade shows.
See industry description for 722330.

This industry prepares and serves meals/snacks for immediate consumption from motorized vehicles or nonmotorized carts (e.g., hot dog carts, ice cream trucks). The establishment is the central location for servicing the route.

This industry group operates establishments known as bars, taverns, nightclubs, or drinking places, primarily preparing and serving alcoholic beverages for immediate consumption. Limited food services may also be provided.
See industry description for 722410.

This industry operates establishments known as bars, taverns, nightclubs, or drinking places, primarily preparing and serving alcoholic beverages for immediate consumption. Limited food services may also be provided.

This industry group provides food services to patrons who order and are served while seated, or who order/select items and pay before eating. It includes full-service, limited-service, cafeterias, grill buffets, and snack/nonalcoholic beverage bars.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in one of the following: (1) providing food services to patrons who order and are served while seated (i.e., waiter/waitress service) and pay after eating; (2) providing food services to patrons who generally order or select items (e.g., at a counter, in a buffet line) and pay before eating; or (3) preparing and/or serving a specialty snack (e.g., ice cream, frozen yogurt, cookies) and/or nonalcoholic beverages (e.g., coffee, juices, sodas) for consumption on or near the premises.

This industry provides food services where patrons order and are served while seated, paying after eating. These establishments may also sell alcoholic beverages, provide carryout, or present live nontheatrical entertainment.

This industry provides food services (excluding snack/nonalcoholic beverage bars) where patrons order/select items and pay before eating. Food/drink can be consumed on premises, taken out, or delivered. Some establishments may also sell alcoholic beverages.

This industry, known as cafeterias, grill buffets, or buffets, prepares and serves meals for immediate consumption using cafeteria-style or buffet serving equipment. Patrons select food/drink from displays in a continuous line or from buffet stations.

This industry prepares and/or serves specialty snacks (e.g., ice cream, frozen yogurt, cookies, popcorn) or nonalcoholic beverages (e.g., coffee, juices, sodas) for consumption on or near premises. Establishments may also sell related products, promoting a unique snack or beverage.

This sector covers a wide range of services not classified elsewhere, including equipment and machinery repair, religious activities, grantmaking, advocacy, personal care, death care, pet care, photofinishing, parking, and household employment. Excludes retailing equipment with repairs.

This subsector restores and maintains machinery, equipment, and other products to working order, categorizing by the type of repair activity. It includes automotive, electronic, commercial/industrial machinery, and personal/household goods repair, excluding repair done by manufacturers or construction trades.

This industry group provides repair and maintenance services for automotive vehicles and trailers. It includes general mechanical/electrical repair, engine repair/replacement, body/paint/interior/glass repair, oil change/lubrication, and car washing.

naics_name: Automotive Mechanical and Electrical Repair and Maintenance
naics_desc: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing mechanical or electrical repair and maintenance services for automotive vehicles, such as passenger cars, trucks, and vans, and all trailers. These establishments may specialize in a single service or may provide a wide range of these services.

This industry provides a wide range of mechanical and electrical repair/maintenance services for automotive vehicles (passenger cars, trucks, vans) and trailers, or performs engine repair/replacement. Examples include auto repair garages and engine repair shops.

This industry provides specialized mechanical or electrical repair/maintenance services (excluding engine repair/replacement) for automotive vehicles and trailers. Examples include automotive brake repair, suspension repair, exhaust system replacement, electrical repair, transmission repair, and tune-up shops.

naics_name: Other Automotive Mechanical and Electrical Repair and Maintenance
naics_desc: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing specialized mechanical or electrical repair and maintenance services (except engine repair and replacement, exhaust systems repair, and transmission repair) for automotive vehicles, such as passenger cars, trucks, and vans, and all trailers. Illustrative Examples: Automotive brake repair shops Automotive radiator repair shops Automotive electrical repair shops Automotive tune-up shops Cross-References. Establishments primarily engaged in--

naics_name: Automotive Body, Paint, Interior, and Glass Repair
naics_desc: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing one or more of the following: (1) repairing or customizing automotive vehicle and trailer bodies and interiors; (2) painting automotive vehicle and trailer bodies; (3) replacing, repairing, and/or tinting automotive vehicle glass; and (4) customizing automobile, truck, and van interiors for the physically disabled or other customers with special requirements.

naics_name: Automotive Body, Paint, and Interior Repair and Maintenance
naics_desc: This industry repairs or customizes automotive vehicle/trailer bodies and interiors, and/or paints automotive vehicles/trailer bodies. It includes automotive body shops, body conversion services, paint shops, and restoration of antique/classic automotive vehicles.
This industry replaces, repairs, and/or tints automotive vehicle glass, e.g., for passenger cars, trucks, and vans.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing automotive repair and maintenance services (except mechanical and electrical repair and maintenance and body, paint, interior, and glass repair) for automotive vehicles, such as passenger cars, trucks, and vans, and all trailers.

naics_name: Automotive Oil Change and Lubrication Shops
naics_desc: This industry changes motor oil and lubricates the chassis of automotive vehicles (passenger cars, trucks, vans).
This industry cleans, washes, and/or waxes automotive vehicles (passenger cars, trucks, vans) and trailers. It includes automotive detail shops, mobile car/truck washes, and self-service car washes.

naics_name: All Other Automotive Repair and Maintenance
naics_desc: This industry provides automotive repair/maintenance services (excluding mechanical/electrical repair, body/paint/interior/glass repair, oil change/lubrication, and car washing) for automotive vehicles and trailers. Examples include automotive air-conditioning repair and rustproofing shops.

naics_name: Electronic and Precision Equipment Repair and Maintenance
naics_desc: This industry group repairs and maintains consumer electronic equipment, computers, office machines, communication equipment, and other electronic/precision equipment/instruments, without retailing new products. Services cover items like microscopes, radar, televisions, and medical equipment.

naics_name: Electronic and Precision Equipment Repair and Maintenance
naics_desc: See industry description for 811210.

naics_name: Electronic and Precision Equipment Repair and Maintenance
naics_desc: This industry repairs/maintains consumer electronic equipment, computers, office machines, communication equipment, and other electronic/precision equipment/instruments, without retailing these products as new. Items include microscopes, radar, televisions, stereos, computers, fax machines, and medical equipment.

naics_name: Computer and Office Machine Repair and Maintenance
naics_desc: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in repairing and maintaining computers and office machines without retailing new computers and office machines, such as photocopying machines; computer terminals, storage devices, and printers; and CD-ROM drives. Cross-References. Establishments primarily engaged in--

This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in repairing and maintaining communication equipment without retailing new communication equipment, such as telephones, fax machines, communications transmission equipment, and two-way radios. Cross-References. Establishments primarily engaged in--

naics_name: Commercial and Industrial Machinery and Equipment (except Automotive and Electronic) Repair and Maintenance
naics_desc: This industry group repairs and maintains commercial and industrial machinery and equipment. Services include sharpening/installing blades/saws, welding, and repairing agricultural, heavy, and industrial machinery, e.g., forklifts, machine tools, and commercial refrigeration equipment.

naics_name: Commercial and Industrial Machinery and Equipment (except Automotive and Electronic) Repair and Maintenance
naics_desc: See industry description for 811310.

naics_name: Commercial and Industrial Machinery and Equipment (except Automotive and Electronic) Repair and Maintenance
naics_desc: This industry repairs/maintains commercial/industrial machinery and equipment. It sharpens/installs commercial/industrial machinery blades/saws, provides welding repair, or repairs agricultural/heavy/industrial machinery (e.g., forklifts, machine tools, commercial refrigeration equipment, construction equipment, mining machinery).

naics_name: Personal and Household Goods Repair and Maintenance
naics_desc: This industry group repairs and services home/garden equipment, household appliances, reupholsters/repairs furniture, and fixes footwear/leather goods. It also covers other personal/household goods like garments, watches, jewelry, musical instruments, and recreational boats.

naics_name: Home and Garden Equipment and Appliance Repair and Maintenance
naics_desc: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in repairing and servicing home and garden equipment and/or household-type appliances without retailing new equipment or appliances. Establishments in this industry repair and maintain items, such as lawnmowers, edgers, snowblowers, leaf blowers, washing machines, clothes dryers, and refrigerators.

naics_name: Home and Garden Equipment Repair and Maintenance
naics_desc: This industry repairs/services home and garden equipment without retailing new items, e.g., lawnmowers, handheld power tools, edgers, snowblowers, leaf blowers, and trimmers.
This industry repairs/services household appliances without retailing new appliances, e.g., refrigerators, stoves, washing machines, clothes dryers, and room air-conditioners.
See industry description for 811420.
This industry reupholsters, refinishes, repairs, and/or restores furniture.
See industry description for 811430.
This industry repairs footwear and/or other leather/leather-like goods (e.g., handbags, briefcases) without retailing new footwear/leather goods.

naics_name: Other Personal and Household Goods Repair and Maintenance
naics_desc: See industry description for 811490.

naics_name: Other Personal and Household Goods Repair and Maintenance
naics_desc: This industry repairs/services personal or household-type goods (excluding home/garden equipment, appliances, furniture, and footwear/leather goods) without retailing new items. It includes repairing garments, watches, jewelry, musical instruments, bicycles/motorcycles, and recreational boats.

This subsector provides personal and laundry services to individuals, households, and businesses. Services include personal care (e.g., hair, nail, skin), death care, drycleaning, laundry, pet care, photofinishing, temporary parking, and dating services.

This industry group provides appearance care services to individual consumers, e.g., hair, nail, and skin care services, permanent makeup, ear piercing, hair replacement/weaving, tanning, and tattoo parlors. Also includes non-medical diet and weight reducing centers.

naics_name: Hair, Nail, and Skin Care Services
naics_desc: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in one or more of the following: (1) providing hair care services; (2) providing nail care services; and (3) providing facials or applying makeup (except permanent makeup).
This industry, known as barber shops or men's hair stylist shops, primarily cuts, trims, and styles men's/boys' hair, and/or shaves and trims men's beards.

This industry (excluding barber shops) cuts, trims, shampoos, colors, waves, or styles hair; provides facials; and/or applies makeup (excluding permanent makeup). Examples include beauty parlors, hairdressing salons, and cosmetology salons.
This industry provides nail care services, e.g., manicures, pedicures, and nail extensions.
This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing personal care services (except hair, nail, facial, or nonpermanent makeup services).

This industry provides non-medical services to assist clients in attaining/maintaining desired weight. Services typically include individual/group counseling, menu/exercise planning, and weight/body measurement monitoring, often integrating weight reduction product sales.

This industry provides personal care services (excluding hair, nail, facial, nonpermanent makeup, or non-medical diet/weight reducing services). Examples include day spas, depilatory/electrolysis salons, ear piercing, hair replacement/weaving, tanning salons, and tattoo parlors.

This industry group prepares the dead for burial/interment, conducts funerals (providing facilities, arranging transportation, selling caskets), operates sites/structures for human/animal remains interment, and/or cremates the dead.
See industry description for 812210.

This industry prepares the dead for burial/interment and conducts funerals (providing facilities for wakes, arranging transportation, selling caskets). Funeral homes combined with crematories are included.
See industry description for 812220.
This industry operates sites/structures reserved for human/animal remains interment and/or cremates the dead. It includes cemetery associations, memorial gardens, pet cemeteries, and mausoleums.

This industry group operates coin- or card-operated self-service laundries/drycleaners, provides drycleaning/laundering services, offers drop-off/pick-up sites, and supplies/services laundered items (e.g., uniforms, linens) on a rental/contract basis.
See industry description for 812310.

This industry operates facilities with coin- or card-operated self-service laundry/drycleaning equipment for customer use on premises, and/or supplies/services such equipment for customer use in other businesses like apartments and dormitories.
See industry description for 812320.

This industry provides drycleaning (excluding coin-operated), laundering (excluding linen/uniform supply, coin-operated), drop-off/pick-up sites, and/or specialty cleaning for garments/textile items (excluding carpets/upholstery). Services may be performed on-premises, off-premises, or both.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in supplying, on a rental or contract basis, laundered items, such as uniforms, gowns and coats, table linens, bed linens, towels, clean room apparel, and treated mops or shop towels.
This industry supplies, on a rental or contract basis, laundered items like table/bed linens, towels, diapers, and uniforms/gowns/coats for various professions.

This industry supplies, on a rental or contract basis, laundered industrial work uniforms, related work clothing (e.g., protective apparel, clean room apparel), and dust control items (e.g., treated mops, rugs, mats, shop towels).

This industry group provides personal services (excluding personal care, death care, drycleaning/laundry, pet care, photofinishing, parking). It includes bail bonding, shoeshine, coin/card-operated personal services machine operations, social escort services, consumer buying, and wedding planning.
See industry description for 812910.
This industry provides pet care services (excluding veterinary), e.g., boarding, grooming, sitting, walking, and training pets.
This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in developing film and/or making photographic slides, prints, and enlargements.
This industry (excluding "one-hour" photofinishing labs) develops film and/or makes photographic slides, prints, and enlargements.
This industry, known as "one-hour" photofinishing labs, develops film and/or makes photographic slides, prints, and enlargements on a short turnaround or while-you-wait basis.
See industry description for 812930.
This industry provides parking space for motor vehicles, usually on an hourly, daily, or monthly basis, and/or valet parking services.
See industry description for 812990.

This industry provides personal services (excluding personal care, death care, drycleaning/laundry, pet care, photofinishing, or parking/valet services). Examples include bail bonding, shoeshine services, coin-operated personal services machine operations, social escort services, consumer buying, and wedding planning.

naics_name: Religious, Grantmaking, Civic, Professional, and Similar Organizations
naics_desc: This subsector groups establishments organizing religious activities, supporting causes through grantmaking, advocating social/political causes, and promoting/defending members' interests. Industry groups are defined by activities like funding causes, advocacy, and membership-based organizations.
This industry group operates religious organizations (churches, temples, mosques, monasteries) and/or administers organized religions or promotes religious activities.
See industry description for 813110.
This industry operates religious organizations (e.g., churches, temples, mosques, monasteries) and/or administers organized religions or promotes religious activities.

This industry group awards grants from trust funds (grantmaking foundations, charitable trusts) and/or raises funds for social welfare activities (health, educational, scientific, cultural), including community chests and federated charities.

This industry comprises (1) establishments known as grantmaking foundations or charitable trusts and (2) establishments primarily engaged in raising funds for a wide range of social welfare activities, such as health, educational, scientific, and cultural activities.

This industry comprises grantmaking foundations or charitable trusts that award grants from trust funds (based on competitive selection or grantor preferences), or fund a single entity. It includes community foundations, philanthropic trusts, corporate foundations, and scholarship trusts.
This industry raises funds for health-related research, e.g., disease prevention (heart, cancer, diabetes), health education, and patient services.

This industry (excluding voluntary health organizations) raises funds for a wide range of social welfare activities, e.g., educational, scientific, cultural, and health. It includes community chest fundraising organizations, federated charities, and united fund councils.

This industry group promotes particular causes or specific social/political goals for broad or specific constituencies. It includes human rights, environmental protection/conservation/wildlife organizations, and other social advocacy (e.g., peace, substance abuse prevention).

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in promoting a particular cause or working for the realization of a specific social or political goal to benefit a broad or specific constituency. These organizations may solicit contributions and offer memberships to support these goals.

This industry promotes human rights causes for broad or specific constituencies, addressing issues like constitutional rights, civil liberties, protection of vulnerable groups, intergroup relations, and voter education. These organizations may solicit contributions and offer memberships.

This industry promotes environmental and wildlife preservation/protection. It addresses issues like clean air/water, global warming, natural resource conservation/development, and protecting wildlife/endangered species. Organizations may solicit contributions and offer memberships.

This industry engages in social advocacy (excluding human rights and environmental protection/conservation/wildlife preservation). It addresses issues like peace, international understanding, community action (excluding civic organizations), and advancing social causes (e.g., firearms safety, drunk driving prevention, drug abuse awareness).

This industry group promotes civic and social interests of its members, e.g., alumni associations, automobile clubs (excluding travel), booster clubs, ethnic associations, fraternal lodges, parent-teacher associations, and veterans' membership organizations.
See industry description for 813410.

This industry promotes civic and social interests of its members, e.g., alumni associations, automobile clubs (excluding travel), booster clubs, ethnic associations, fraternal lodges, parent-teacher associations, scouting organizations, social clubs, and veterans' membership organizations.

naics_name: Business, Professional, Labor, Political, and Similar Organizations
naics_desc: This industry group promotes members' interests (excluding religious, social advocacy, civic/social organizations). It includes business associations, professional organizations, labor unions, and political organizations, which may conduct research, develop statistics, lobby officials, or publish newsletters.
See industry description for 813910.

This industry promotes the business interests of its members. Activities include research on new products/services, market statistics, quality/certification standards, lobbying, and publishing newsletters/books/periodicals for members.
See industry description for 813920.

This industry promotes professional interests of its members and the profession as a whole. Activities include research, statistics, quality/certification standards, lobbying, and publishing newsletters/books/periodicals for members.

naics_name: Labor Unions and Similar Labor Organizations
naics_desc: See industry description for 813930.

naics_name: Labor Unions and Similar Labor Organizations
naics_desc: This industry promotes the interests of organized labor and union employees.
See industry description for 813940.
This industry promotes the interests of national, state, or local political parties or candidates. It includes political groups organized to raise funds for parties or individual candidates.

naics_name: Other Similar Organizations (except Business, Professional, Labor, and Political Organizations)
naics_desc: See industry description for 813990.

naics_name: Other Similar Organizations (except Business, Professional, Labor, and Political Organizations)
naics_desc: This industry promotes members' interests (excluding religious, social advocacy, civic/social, business associations, professional organizations, labor unions, and political organizations). Examples include athletic associations/leagues (regulatory), condominium/homeowners' associations, and property/tenants'/cooperative owners' associations.
This subsector includes private households employing workers on or about the premises for household operation, e.g., cooks, maids, nannies, gardeners, and other maintenance staff.

This industry group comprises private households employing workers on or about the premises for household operation, e.g., cooks, maids, nannies, butlers, non-medical personal care aides, gardeners, and maintenance workers.
See industry description for 814110.

This industry comprises private households primarily employing workers on or about the premises for household operation, e.g., cooks, maids, nannies, butlers, non-medical personal care aides, gardeners, caretakers, and other maintenance workers.
[WHD Internal] State-level government entities tracked by WHD.
[WHD Internal] State correctional and detention facilities.
[WHD Internal] State correctional and detention facilities.
[WHD Internal] State-operated hospital facilities.
[WHD Internal] State-operated hospital facilities.

This sector consists of government agencies (federal, state, local) that administer public programs with executive, legislative, or judicial authority. Activities include setting policy, creating laws, adjudicating cases, and providing public safety/national defense, generally for programs not performed by private establishments.

naics_name: Executive, Legislative, and Other General Government Support
naics_desc: This subsector groups offices of government executives, legislative bodies, public finance, and general government support functions. It includes central administrative offices, legislative commissions, and agencies providing general government support.

naics_name: Executive, Legislative, and Other General Government Support
naics_desc: This industry group serves as offices of chief executives, legislative bodies, and their advisory committees/commissions. It includes public finance, taxation, monetary policy, debt/investment administration, auditing, and government employee retirement trust fund administration.
See industry description for 921110.
This industry comprises government establishments serving as offices of chief executives and their advisory committees/commissions, e.g., offices of the president, governors, and mayors.
See industry description for 921120.

This industry comprises government establishments serving as legislative bodies and their advisory committees/commissions, e.g., Congress, state legislatures, and advisory/study legislative commissions.
See industry description for 921130.

This industry includes government establishments primarily engaged in public finance, taxation, and monetary policy. Activities cover financial administration, tax administration/collection, fund custody/disbursement, debt/investment administration, auditing, and government employee retirement trust fund administration.
See industry description for 921140.

This industry comprises government establishments serving as councils/boards of commissioners or supervisors where the chief executive (e.g., county executive, city mayor) is a member of the legislative body.

naics_name: American Indian and Alaska Native Tribal Governments
naics_desc: See industry description for 921150.

naics_name: American Indian and Alaska Native Tribal Governments
naics_desc: This industry comprises American Indian and Alaska Native governing bodies performing legislative, judicial, and administrative functions for their lands. It includes tribal councils, courts, and law enforcement.
See industry description for 921190.

This industry comprises government establishments primarily providing general support for government (excluding elsewhere classified public administration). Services include personnel services, election boards, civil rights commissions, and supply agencies.
State higher education institutions.
[Retired] The Justice, Public Order, and Safety Activities subsector groups government establishments engaged in the administration of justice, public order, and safety programs.

naics_name: Justice, Public Order, and Safety Activities
naics_desc: This industry group involves government establishments administering justice, public order, and safety programs. It includes civilian courts, police protection, legal counsel/prosecution, correctional institutions, parole/probation offices, and fire protection.
See industry description for 922110.

This industry comprises civilian courts of law (excluding American Indian and Alaska Native tribal courts). It includes civilian courts, courts of law, and sheriffs' offices performing court functions only.
See industry description for 922120.

This industry comprises government establishments primarily engaged in criminal/civil law enforcement, police, traffic safety, and related activities for law enforcement/order preservation. Combined police and fire departments are included.
See industry description for 922130.

This industry comprises government establishments primarily providing legal counsel or prosecution services for the government. Examples include attorney generals' offices, district attorneys' offices, and public defenders'/prosecutors' offices.
See industry description for 922140.

This industry comprises government establishments primarily managing/operating correctional institutions (e.g., penitentiaries, detention centers, prisons, jails) for the confinement, correction, and rehabilitation of adult and/or juvenile offenders.
See industry description for 922150.
This industry comprises government establishments judicially administering probation offices, parole offices and boards, and pardon boards.
See industry description for 922160.

This industry comprises government establishments primarily engaged in firefighting and other related fire protection activities. Government establishments providing combined fire protection and ambulance or rescue services are included.

naics_name: Other Justice, Public Order, and Safety Activities
naics_desc: See industry description for 922190.

naics_name: Other Justice, Public Order, and Safety Activities
naics_desc: This industry comprises government establishments in public order and safety (excluding courts, police, legal counsel/prosecution, correctional, parole/probation, fire protection). It includes general administration of public order/safety programs, consumer product safety commissions, and emergency planning/management offices.

This subsector groups government establishments administering human resource programs, including education, public health, and veterans' affairs. It covers planning, supervision, and coordination of these social programs.

This industry group involves government establishments planning, administering, and coordinating human resource programs, e.g., education, public health, and veterans' affairs. It includes social security, disability insurance, Medicare, unemployment insurance, and workers' compensation programs.
See industry description for 923110.

This industry comprises government establishments centrally coordinating, planning, supervising, and administering funds, policies, intergovernmental activities, statistical reports, data collection, and centralized programs for educational administration. Government scholarship programs are included.
See industry description for 923120.

This industry comprises government establishments planning, administering, and coordinating public health programs/services, including environmental health, mental health, categorical health programs, health statistics, and immunization services. It includes public health-related inspections and coroners' offices.

naics_name: Administration of Human Resource Programs (except Education, Public Health, and Veterans' Affairs Programs)
naics_desc: See industry description for 923130.

naics_name: Administration of Human Resource Programs (except Education, Public Health, and Veterans' Affairs Programs)
naics_desc: This industry comprises government establishments planning, administering, and coordinating programs for public assistance, social work, and welfare activities. It includes the administration of Social Security, disability insurance, Medicare, unemployment insurance, and workers' compensation programs.
See industry description for 923140.

This industry comprises government establishments administering programs of assistance, training, counseling, and other services to veterans and their dependents/heirs/survivors. It includes Veterans' Affairs offices that liaise and coordinate with other service organizations and government agencies.

This subsector groups government establishments administering environmental quality programs. It focuses on regulating air/water resources, solid waste, pollution control, flood control, drainage development, toxic waste cleanup, and coordinating these activities.

This industry group includes government establishments administering, regulating, and enforcing air/water resource programs, solid waste management, pollution control, flood control, drainage development, toxic waste removal, and coordinating these activities.

naics_name: Administration of Air and Water Resource and Solid Waste Management Programs
naics_desc: See industry description for 924110.

naics_name: Administration of Air and Water Resource and Solid Waste Management Programs
naics_desc: This industry administers, regulates, and enforces air/water resource programs; administers/regulates solid waste management, water/air pollution control/prevention, flood control, drainage development, water resource consumption, and toxic waste removal/cleanup programs; and coordinates these activities at intergovernmental levels.
See industry description for 924120.

This industry administers, regulates, supervises, and controls land use (including recreational areas); conserves/preserves natural resources (erosion control, geological survey); administers weather forecasting programs; and protects public/private forest lands. It also plans/manages/regulates game, fish, and wildlife populations.
State parks and recreation programs.

[Retired] The Administration of Housing Programs, Urban Planning, and Community Development subsector groups government establishments primarily engaged in the administration of housing, urban planning, and community development.

naics_name: Administration of Housing Programs, Urban Planning, and Community Development
naics_desc: This industry group involves government establishments administering and planning housing programs, urban planning, and community/rural development. It includes land redevelopment agencies, regional planning, and zoning boards.
See industry description for 925110.
This industry comprises government establishments primarily engaged in the administration and planning of housing programs.

naics_name: Administration of Urban Planning and Community and Rural Development
naics_desc: See industry description for 925120.

naics_name: Administration of Urban Planning and Community and Rural Development
naics_desc: This industry comprises government establishments administering and planning urban/rural area development, including land redevelopment agencies, regional planning, and zoning boards/commissions.
State highway and transportation departments.
[Retired] This subsector comprises government establishments primarily engaged in the administration of economic programs.

This industry group includes government establishments administering, promoting, and developing economic resources, e.g., business, industry, and tourism. It covers general statistical data, economic well-being, and regulation/licensing/inspection of various commercial sectors.
See industry description for 926110.

This industry comprises government establishments administering, promoting, and developing economic resources, e.g., business, industry, and tourism. It covers general statistical data, economic well-being, consumer protection, and small business development.

naics_name: Regulation and Administration of Transportation Programs
naics_desc: See industry description for 926120.

naics_name: Regulation and Administration of Transportation Programs
naics_desc: This industry comprises government establishments administering, regulating, licensing, planning, inspecting, and investigating transportation services and facilities. It includes motor vehicle/operator licensing, the Coast Guard (excluding academy), and parking authorities.

naics_name: Regulation and Administration of Communications, Electric, Gas, and Other Utilities
naics_desc: See industry description for 926130.

naics_name: Regulation and Administration of Communications, Electric, Gas, and Other Utilities
naics_desc: This industry comprises government establishments administering, regulating, licensing, and inspecting utilities, e.g., communications, electric power (fossil, nuclear, solar, water, wind), gas, water supply, and sewerage.

naics_name: Regulation of Agricultural Marketing and Commodities
naics_desc: See industry description for 926140.

naics_name: Regulation of Agricultural Marketing and Commodities
naics_desc: This industry comprises government establishments planning, administering, and coordinating agricultural programs for production, marketing, and utilization, including educational/promotional activities. It regulates/controls grading and inspection of food, plants, animals, and other agricultural products.

naics_name: Regulation, Licensing, and Inspection of Miscellaneous Commercial Sectors
naics_desc: See industry description for 926150.

naics_name: Regulation, Licensing, and Inspection of Miscellaneous Commercial Sectors
naics_desc: This industry comprises government establishments regulating, licensing, and inspecting commercial sectors, e.g., retail trade, professional occupations, manufacturing, mining, construction, and services. It maintains physical standards, regulates hazardous conditions, and enforces alcoholic beverage control regulations.
State transit systems.
[Retired] This subsector comprises government establishments that conduct space research.
This industry group comprises government establishments administering and operating space flights, space research, and space exploration, including space flight centers.
See industry description for 927110.
This industry comprises government establishments administering and operating space flights, space research, and space exploration, including space flight centers.
This subsector groups government establishments focusing on national security (Armed Forces, National Guard) and international affairs, including programs relating to other nations and peoples.

This industry group includes government establishments of the Armed Forces and National Guard focusing on national security, and establishments primarily engaged in international affairs and programs relating to other nations.
See industry description for 928110.

This industry comprises government establishments of the Armed Forces, including the National Guard, primarily engaged in national security and related activities, e.g., military police, military training schools (excluding academies), and military courts.
See industry description for 928120.
This industry comprises establishments of U.S. and foreign governments primarily engaged in international affairs and programs relating to other nations and peoples.

[WHD Internal] Local school boards and districts.
